plugins.name: rockstarr-content
plugins.description: Rockstarr AI long-form content drafting plugin. Four publishing lanes (researched blog, thought leadership, email newsletter, LinkedIn newsletter) gated by the client's stack cadence, plus interview-driven case studies and repurposed derivatives. v0.7 removes the LinkedIn polls lane introduced in v0.6 — polls moved to the new rockstarr-social plugin where they fit alongside other short-form social content. v0.5 added the strategic content-planning layer: seo-strategy outputs a canonical backlog of 25-32 prioritized blog topics organized into 4-5 pillar+supporting clusters; ideate-topics + content-calendar + outline-blog are backlog-aware. Researched-blog lane (v0.4) runs WebSearch research + 13-item SEO/GEO checklist as Pass 1 before stop-slop. Thought-leadership lane (v0.3) runs the canonical TL rubric as Pass 1 before stop-slop. Every lane drafts in the client's approved voice from first-party KB; third-party sources are cited inline and listed in References, never paraphrased as the client's voice. Requires rockstarr-infra v0.8.2+ for the shared blog-seo-geo reference; v0.8.1+ for the TL rubric; v0.4+ for the rest.

plugins.name: rockstarr-infra
plugins.description: Rockstarr AI infrastructure plugin — the foundation every Rockstarr bot depends on. Scaffolds each client's /rockstarr-ai/ folder, runs the guided intake interview (or the legacy workbook flow) to produce a canonical client-profile.md, generates the brand voice and style guide, ingests the knowledge base, captures the tool stack, and owns the approve and publish-log lifecycle every downstream plugin uses. Ships the cross-bot notification layer — approvals-digest (daily client email), approvals-backlog-alert (weekly strategist email when the queue is heavy), and notify-reply-ready (urgent client email when an outreach reply lands) — on top of the rockstarr-mailer Worker, with scheduled tasks wired at scaffold time. Exposes shared references the content, social, outreach, reply, and ops bots consume: the thought-leadership rubric, the blog SEO + GEO checklist, and the mandatory stop-slop pass. The entry point for every new client is run-onboarding; nothing else in the Rockstarr AI stack runs until this plugin has done its work.

plugins.name: rockstarr-ops
plugins.description: Rockstarr AI sales-ops bot for the Growth OS. Tool-agnostic by design: every CRM, task system, meeting recorder, calendar, email platform, deliverability tool, and LinkedIn outreach tool is named in stack.md at install time. V0.1 covers daily call prep (orchestrator), Call 1 + Call 2 prep, client catch-up agendas, old-lead audits, lead reengagement, post-call transcript processing, and recurring email-deliverability tests. Drafting goes through rockstarr-reply; CRM writes go through the active rockstarr-ops-bot-<crm> variant; LinkedIn DM sends go through the active rockstarr-outreach-* variant. No baked-in framework, voice, pitch, or pricing — every behavior comes from intake-produced files (pitch.md, ops-call-framework.md, deliverability-config.md, icp-qualifications.md, style-guide.md) plus stack.md.

plugins.name: rockstarr-outreach-interceptly
plugins.description: Rockstarr AI LinkedIn outreach plugin for clients who run Interceptly. Owns the Interceptly integration end-to-end: account discovery, per-account persona capture, campaign lifecycle (draft, launch, pause, stop), multi-account session confirmation, the daily inbox + tasks loop, per-reply channel I/O (send, label, follow-up task, meeting proposal, booking), and outreach-side metrics + weekly reporting. Hands every inbound reply to rockstarr-reply for drafting and operator approval, then executes the returned bundle inside Interceptly. Exposes a daily-loop orchestration skill that scaffold-client wires to the schedule for background runs; in background mode, drafts are staged but never sent inline and the loop fires one urgent reply-ready email per run via rockstarr-infra:notify-reply-ready (>=0.8.0) when new reply drafts get staged.

plugins.name: rockstarr-outreach-salesnav
plugins.description: Rockstarr AI LinkedIn outreach plugin for clients who will not install Interceptly / MeetAlfred / Dripify / Waalaxy. Supports two campaign types — full-sequence (connect + three-message post-accept conversation) and connect-only (connection requests only, no post-accept sequence) — selected at draft time and validated at registration. Plans multi-touch campaigns against a client-supplied ICP and saved Sales Navigator search, executes the daily outreach loop directly in Sales Nav via Chrome MCP using the row-level three-dot menu method with explicit skip-rule handling, and keeps all state in a single workbook inside /rockstarr-ai/. Enforces a 20/day + 100/week connection cap across all active campaigns regardless of type, routes every reply through rockstarr-reply for approval, fires a single urgent client email per detect-replies run summarizing every newly-staged reply draft (via rockstarr-infra:notify-reply-ready), and confirms the browser is signed in to the correct LinkedIn before every run. Page-follow invites moved to the rockstarr-social plugin in 0.2.0 — install that alongside this one if you also want monthly page-growth automation.

plugins.name: rockstarr-reply
plugins.description: Rockstarr AI channel-agnostic reply drafting for the Growth OS. Called by every rockstarr-outreach-* plugin to draft replies to inbound LinkedIn messages. Owns voice, classification, three-option non-ICP flow, stop-slop pass, and the 'send it' authorization gate. The caller owns the channel I/O. v0.2 wires the front-matter contract for rockstarr-infra v0.8 cross-bot notifications (notify-reply-ready + approvals-digest).

plugins.name: rockstarr-social
plugins.description: Rockstarr AI social plugin. Drafts daily/weekly LinkedIn (and X / IG) short-form posts in the client's approved voice, builds a balanced weekly batch, and exports to the client's scheduler (Publer default; GrowthAmp planner on demand). Bot does not post directly — every batch flows through the standard rockstarr-infra approve path, then a manually-triggered export skill writes a Publer-shaped CSV to /05_published-staging/social/. v0.1 also absorbs three formerly-adjacent surfaces: draft-polls (the LinkedIn polls lane, moved out of rockstarr-content where it shipped in v0.6 — short-form polls are a social lane, not a content lane), invite-page-followers (the monthly LinkedIn page-follow invite run, moved out of rockstarr-outreach-salesnav — page growth is a social-audience task, not an outreach campaign), and li-comment-check (a new daily skill for monitoring + responding to comments on managed LinkedIn accounts in the account holder's voice; multi-account rotation, ClickUp task closure optional). Per-week post count and mix ratios are config-driven in stack.md (social_posts_per_week + social_mix). Requires rockstarr-infra v0.8.2+ for the shared stop-slop pass and approvals lifecycle.
